financial.note: Holds ~51% stake; PTT is the primary vehicle for Thailand's national energy security and fiscal revenue (dividends).
Holds ~1.57% stake (via OR) and significant indirect holdings in PTT; aligns with government-linked investment stability.
Maintains a 'BBB+' rating for PTT but revised the outlook to 'Negative' in April 2026, tracking the Thai sovereign outlook.

supply_chain.note: Primary driver of group earnings; PTT owns ~65%. Performance is directly tied to global crude oil and natural gas price cycles.
PTT serves as the primary feedstock supplier (crude/gas) and off-taker; performance is sensitive to refining margins and petrochem spreads.
PTT owns 75%; critical for non-oil growth via Café Amazon and EV charging infrastructure transition.
Partnership to strengthen Asian LNG market competitiveness and secure natural gas supply for Thailand's power sector.

equity_cross_holdings.note: The group's power flagship focusing on renewable energy and battery value chains.

competitors.note: Competes in international oil trading and regional E&P bidding.
Primary domestic rival in the refining and retail station segment (following the Esso acquisition).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Directly impacts PTTEP's ASP and the group's inventory valuation; 2026 forecast revolves around $60-65/bbl.
As a major exporter and USD-denominated earner (E&P), a weaker THB boosts translated earnings but inflates LNG import costs.
Decisions on natural gas pricing structures (Pool Gas) and FT tariffs directly impact gas business margins.
